Thought 5: The narrative states that Irving stayed later than Uma on the night of the murder, and Addison was present at the time of the murder. However, the murder weapon was a vial of acid. Irving has a direct link to corrosive substances through his research and purchases, and he was seen carrying a vial. Addison, while having a motive (Uma catching him stealing) and opportunity (present at the time), has no established connection to the acid. The custom-made suit suggests Addison might have stolen equipment, but it doesn't directly tie him to the murder weapon. Irving's motive (plagiarism threat) is also strong, and his behavior (carrying a vial, purchasing corrosives) is highly suspicious. The evidence points more strongly to Irving.
